carbon_policy_signal.item: EU Commission weakens methane enforcement rules under US pressure; energy-security exemptions for oil firms breach authorization.
carbon_policy_signal.why_it_matters: Relaxed methane rules reduce compliance cost for gas producers; marginal upside to gas supply economics dampens fuel-switch incentive, indirectly supporting EUA via lower coal displacement.

freshness_caveat: Coal data stale as of 2025-12-26 (133 days old); Clean Dark stale as of 2025-12-31 (128 days old). GB Power, Clean Spark, DE Power, TTF, EUA, Storage, and Renewables are current as of 2026-05-07/08.
risk_flags: Storage at 34.5% — 12.4 pp below seasonal — refill pace critical to summer power curve.
Renewable share collapsed 32.8% daily to 27%; thermal call extended; watch reversal risk.
Hormuz reopening by Trump admin would ease LNG/crude inflows to NW Europe — downside tail for TTF and power.
Coal data stale (133 days old); Clean Dark stale (128 days); GB/DE/CS prices current as of 2026-05-08.

base.summary: Renewables stabilize; GB and Clean Spark drift lower on normalization; TTF flat around 43–44 EUR/MWh.

downside.summary: Hormuz reopening progresses; Libya ramp accelerates; Renewable output rebounds; storage refill pace eases pressure.

upside.summary: Hormuz closure escalation confirmed; Iran supply disruption widens; LNG scarcity premium reasserts on TTF and power.

themes: GB Power extended despite daily weakness; thermal baseload tight
Clean Spark elevated on low renewables (18th-pctile); coal deeply depressed (8th-pctile)
Storage 12.4 pp below seasonal; Hormuz geopolitical tail-risk reasserts
Methane rule relaxation and Libya LNG alternatives weigh on TTF
top_drivers: Renewables collapsed 41.24% on week; 18th-pctile pushes thermal baseload (Clean Spark 92nd-pctile) and GB power to 93rd-pctile.
Storage at 11th-pctile (34.5%, 12.4 pp below seasonal) tightens gas-to-power arbitrage; TTF flat but curve flattening risk if refill pace disappoints.
Geopolitical: Hormuz reopening risk (Trump admin push) + Libya supply ramp weaken TTF bear case; Iran war premium may unwind if corridor restores flows.
top_takeaway: GB Power at 93rd-percentile despite -8% selloff; Clean Spark 92nd-pctile on tight renewable supply; Hormuz reopening risk and Libya gas pivotal for TTF bear case.

watchlist.why: Trump administration mobilization to reopen Strait; reopening would ease Persian Gulf LNG/crude flows, bearish for European power and TTF.

watchlist.metric: Libya-Italy gas pipeline utilization and production ramp
watchlist.why: Italy seeking alternative to Iran-war-disrupted ME supply; slow ramp-up limits TTF support but signals regional diversification.

watchlist.metric: EU methane rule exemption implementation scope
watchlist.why: Relaxed enforcement under US pressure reduces compliance cost for gas producers; marginal bear for TTF but signals EU-US policy shift.
27% of load (18th-pctile); 41.24% weekly drop drove Clean Spark to 92nd-pctile; any rebound flattens thermal merit order.

watchlist_dated.name: Hormuz corridor operational status update
watchlist_dated.why: Trump administration actively mobilizing; any confirmed reopening eases LNG/crude flows, bearish power and TTF.
